title: Tesla puts Starlink antenna in Cybercab
summary:
Tesla and Starlink say SpaceX’s satellite internet is now “directly integrated” into the Cybercab, Tesla’s dedicated robotaxi.

The reveal came through a pair of posts on X on Monday, with a cutaway diagram showing a Starlink antenna sitting in the vehicle’s roof. Neither company explained why a car that drives itself on its own computer needs internet from space.

title: The Hyundai IONIQ 5 is flying off dealer lots, and it’s getting harder to find
summary: The IONIQ 5 is a hot seller right now, and Hyundai dealers are reportedly running out of inventory.

title: The US’s first UL 3700 balcony solar microinverter is here
summary: Hoymiles has launched the HiFlow Pro, the first US microinverter certified to meet UL 3700, North America’s new safety standard for plug-in balcony solar.

title: Tesla ‘Full Self-Driving’ got me a speeding ticket, and Tesla killed the fix
summary:
I got a speeding ticket this weekend while using Tesla’s Full Self-Driving. The car was driving itself in Standard mode on FSD when it took me up to 78 km/h in a 50 km/h zone.

A police officer coming the other way clocked me on radar and pulled me over on the spot. I’d turned FSD on less than two minutes earlier.

title: Hyundai’s $5B battery plant opens with enough capacity to power 300,000 EVs a year
summary: Hyundai opened its new $5 billion EV battery plant in Georgia with enough capacity to power about 300,000 electric vehicles a year. The first of which will be used in the three-row IONIQ 9.

title: EGO 21-inch electric Select Cut and Touch Drive mower + 2x batteries new $527 low, Jackery power stations up to 50% off, more
summary:
Leading this week’s Monday Green Deals is a new all-time low price on the popular EGO Power+ 56V 21-inch Cordless Electric Select Cut and Touch Drive Self-Propelled Lawn Mower with 2x 4.0Ah batteries for $527 (saving you $252). Right behind that, we have Jackery’s latest Summer Sale lineup with up to 50% power station discounts, like the new 1,024Wh HomePower 1000 v2 Portable Power Station at $549. There’s also the Baseus X1 Pro Dual-Tracking Outdoor Wireless Solar Security Camera at $130, the Autel MaxiCharger Home Level 2 50A Smart EV Charger at $424, and more waiting for you below. As always on Mondays, you can find all the hangover deals from last week down at the bottom of the page in our latest Electrified Weekly roundup edition.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Ford’s new EV pickup caught at a Tesla Supercharger
summary: Ford’s upcoming midsize electric pickup will start at around $30,000 with a native NACS port to recharge at Tesla Superchargers.

title: Principia is here to win, and it won’t hesitate to help others do the same
summary:
The sun has just about risen on this year’s Electrek Formula Sun Grand Prix (FSGP), the pre-qualifying race before the renowned American Solar Challenge. FSGP officially kicks off tomorrow morning for the teams that have passed rigorous safety and quality checks, known as “scrutineering.”

Over the past few weeks, I’ve had the privilege of speaking directly with teams from around the world, including the Delft Solar Team from the Netherlands and ÉTS Éclipse from Montreal. But for my final profile, I sat down with students from a small liberal arts school just outside St. Louis, right along the Mississippi River. Principia College has a long-running solar car program, which is astonishing considering the school is in the middle of phasing out its only engineering program.

So it was no surprise that after a few minutes speaking with the team’s leadership, I realized the car might end up being the least interesting thing about them.

title: Will we soon see sodium-ion batteries on electric bikes?
summary: For the last couple of years, sodium-ion batteries have been one of the most talked-about “next big things” in the battery world. They promise lower costs, improved safety, and reduced dependence on critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and nickel. Naturally, that has led many riders to wonder whether the next generation of electric bicycles could ditch lithium altogether.

title: Genesis may bring a more affordable luxury EV to the mass market
summary: Genesis is going on the offensive as the Korean luxury brand expands into new segments.

title: PSA: Don't Use An Old Charging Adapter For Your Rivian R2. It May Melt
summary: Since Rivian stuck with 400 volts to keep costs down, the R2's high-amperage charging can be too much for some older adapters.

title: Volkswagen's E-Bike Tries To Keep You Safe With A Rearview Camera And Smart Helmet
summary: The Smart Helmet pairs with Smart Glasses to give the rider live navigation, traffic, and safety alerts.

title: Bentley's EV Will Use an Orchestra to Replicate the Sound of a V8
summary: Bentley realized that rhythm is the most important aspect of engine noise, so it mainly uses drums to replicate the thrum of a V8.

title: Ferrari Is Auctioning Off The First Example Of its First Electric Car. How Much Do You Think It'll Fetch?
summary: Ferrari will be auctioning off the first example of its first production battery-electric car to whoever has the deepest pockets.

title: The Xpeng L03 Aims To Take On Europe And Undercut Tesla
summary: This Chinese SUV has integrated Google Maps and FSD-like capability, all for about $3,500 cheaper than a Tesla Model Y.

title: Toyota Is Paying EV Drivers $3,000 To Switch To A bZ
summary: Toyota is dangling a $3,000 incentive in front of existing EV owners if they make the move to a bZ.

title: 'Completely Insane': China Has 650 New Car Models So Far This Year
summary: China is averaging around 30 brand-new cars every single month—the U.S. does that in a year.

title: BYD Wants To Overtake Toyota, And It Says It Doesn't Need America To Do It
summary: BYD wants to be the top-selling car company in the world within the next five years—with or without selling in the U.S.

title: MG Is Putting Semi-Solid Batteries In Its Next Plug-In Hybrids
summary: The company had already announced that it will soon also introduce its first pure EV with a semi-solid-state battery in Europe.

title: Launch Mode Actually Makes The 2026 Tesla Model Y Performance Slower
summary: The newest Model Y Performance is faster to 60 mph when not using launch mode. What gives?
